data.content:
The bug is on line 29 — `ProcessPayment` only checks for zero, not negative amounts. The fastest iteration workflow for .NET is `dotnet watch test`, which auto-rebuilds and re-runs on every file save.

Let me fix the bug and set up the watch:

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` only checks for `amount == 0` but doesn't check for negative amounts. The fix is to add `amount <= 0` or add a separate check for negative amounts.

The user is asking about the fastest workflow for iterating. The best approach for .NET is `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically on file save, no manual rebuild needed.

Let me fix the bug and set up the watch workflow.

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-36f05ddf69c34567972643efeb3e9f05/PaymentProcessorTests.cs", "old_str": "        if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException(\"Amount cannot be zero\");\n        // Missing: validation for negative amounts", "new_str": "        if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException(\"Amount must be positive\");"}

toolArgs.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
toolArgs.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-36f05ddf69c34567972643efeb3e9f05 && dotnet test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1 | tail -20", "description": "Run the failing test", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content: All 3 tests pass. Here's the final file:

**Fix:** Changed `amount == 0` to `amount <= 0` on line 30, which now catches both zero and negative amounts.

**For fast iteration going forward**, use `dotnet watch test`:

```bash
dotnet watch test --filter "ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException"
```

This watches for file changes and automatically rebuilds + re-runs the filtered test on every save — no manual rebuild needed. Drop the `--filter` to watch all tests.
